runs.output: RISC (Reduced Instruction Set Computer) and CISC (Complex Instruction Set Computer) represent two fundamentally different philosophies in processor design. RISC emphasizes simplicity, predictability, and efficiency, while CISC focuses on functionality and code density. The original RISC designs, exemplified by MIPS, SPARC, and ARM, were built around the principle of a small, fixed-length instruction set that supports a limited number of operations.
